typecheck.output: mypy module not installed in venv

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that E2E tests for Flet apps use component-level mocking (unittest.mock.MagicMock) rather than browser-based Playwright automation, because Flet's CanvasKit renderer doesn't expose DOM elements. Tests import screen classes directly and test them as Python objects, not via page.goto() calls.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All three reviewed features (e2e-home-page, e2e-profile-page, e2e-navigation-sidebar) use mock-based component testing. test_home_page.py docstring explicitly explains: 'This approach tests the Flet screen components directly without requiring a browser/Playwright, which is necessary because Flet's CanvasKit renderer doesn't expose DOM elements.'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a testing strategy note clarifying that unit tests live in src/tests/ (609 passing) while E2E tests use component-level mocking rather than browser automation, due to Flet CanvasKit limitations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: services.yaml shows test command as 'cd src && ./venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v' but actual tests are at src/tests/ not tests/. Lint errors found in .factory/ missions directory are unrelated to main-app milestone.

notes.testPathCorrection: services.yaml specifies 'pytest tests/' but actual test path is 'src/tests/'. This is a pre-existing issue, not introduced by main-app features.
notes.mypyNotInstalled: mypy is not installed in the venv. This is a pre-existing environment issue, not a code issue.
notes.reviewCount: Only 1 of 3 review reports was successfully written by first round of subagents (e2e-profile-page). e2e-home-page and e2e-navigation-sidebar reports were written on re-run. All three reviews passed.
